refined_fr.refined_fr: Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR bundles three distinct obligations: (1) terrain analysis, (2) obstacle identification, and (3) work-zone representation. Each is a separate functional concern with different data sources, processing logic, and stakeholder consumption patterns. The refined FR is solution-neutral (avoids 'mapping/modeling' terminology which implies implementation choice) and focuses on the core problem: making worksite geometry and features available in a structured, queryable form. Splitting into atomic sub-FRs allows independent DP selection and IM packaging.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Confirm that a worksite model exists in a defined schema (e.g., point cloud, mesh, or feature database) and can be queried by downstream planning and execution functions to retrieve spatial attributes (position, geometry, classification) for an agreed set of site features.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-006.1: Acquire terrain elevation and surface geometry data from the worksite.
FR-NGHE-006.2: Identify and classify static obstacles (structures, vegetation, utilities) within the worksite boundary.
FR-NGHE-006.3: Define and represent work-zone boundaries and restricted areas in the spatial model.
FR-NGHE-006.4: Persist and provide access to the worksite spatial model for planning and execution functions.

candidate_dps: DP1: Acquire spatial feature data via multi-modal sensing (vision, range, inertial) and fuse into a unified coordinate frame; store as a structured spatial graph with feature attributes (geometry, classification, safety zone) queryable by downstream planning/execution modules.

DP2: Decompose worksite into discrete zones; assign feature capture responsibility to zone-specific acquisition agents; merge zone models via spatial registration and conflict resolution; maintain a federated spatial database with zone ownership metadata.

DP3: Capture features incrementally through operator-guided annotation of pre-existing site imagery/blueprints; validate against operational policies; store as a rule-based spatial knowledge base with feature constraints and safety rule bindings.

DP4: Stream raw sensor observations into a real-time feature extraction pipeline; classify and localize features using learned models; publish feature updates to a time-versioned spatial model accessible to planning/execution via standardized query interface.

DP5: Establish a hybrid approach: use operator-provided baseline site model (blueprint/CAD) as prior; overlay real-time sensor observations to detect deviations and dynamic features; maintain a delta-encoded spatial model with confidence scores per feature.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP5: Establish a hybrid approach: use operator-provided baseline site model (blueprint/CAD) as prior; overlay real-time sensor observations to detect deviations and dynamic features; maintain a delta-encoded spatial model with confidence scores per feature.

potential_sources: Dependency on baseline model format/availability (mitigated by accepting multiple input formats: CAD, raster imagery, point clouds).
Tight coupling between feature extraction logic and safety policy validation (mitigated by separating feature representation from policy rules; policies query features, not vice versa).
Real-time sensor stream coupling to model update frequency (mitigated by decoupling acquisition rate from model publication rate via buffering/batching).
Confidence scoring scheme must be interpretable to planning/execution (mitigated by defining confidence semantics upfront and versioning the scoring model).
coupling_check.notes: DP5 balances pragmatism (leverages existing site documentation) with adaptability (real-time updates for dynamic features). Decoupling is achieved by: (1) treating baseline model as optional input, not mandatory; (2) separating feature representation (DP) from safety policy enforcement (downstream FR); (3) using a query interface rather than direct module-to-module calls. Risk: if baseline model is unavailable or stale, system degrades to DP4 (real-time only), which is acceptable fallback.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Operator-provided baseline site model (blueprint, CAD, or prior survey) is available at mission start or can be rapidly acquired. RISK: If unavailable, system must fall back to pure real-time sensing (DP4), increasing latency and computational load. MITIGATION: Define fallback acquisition protocol; pre-stage baseline models during mission planning.

ASSUMPTION: Real-time sensor observations can be registered to baseline coordinate frame with acceptable accuracy (e.g., <0.5 m for safety-critical features). RISK: Registration drift over time or in GPS-denied environments. MITIGATION: Implement loop-closure detection and periodic re-registration; use inertial/visual odometry with bounded drift.

ASSUMPTION: Feature extraction models (for detecting deviations and dynamic features) are pre-trained or rapidly trainable on worksite imagery. RISK: Model generalization failure on novel site types or lighting conditions. MITIGATION: Curate diverse training data; implement online adaptation or operator feedback loops.

ASSUMPTION: Confidence scores are meaningful to downstream planning/execution modules. RISK: Misinterpretation of confidence (e.g., treating low-confidence features as absent vs. uncertain). MITIGATION: Define confidence semantics formally (e.g., Bayesian posterior, detection recall); document in interface specification.

ASSUMPTION: Safety policies can be expressed as constraints on feature attributes (geometry, classification, zone membership). RISK: Complex policies requiring temporal reasoning or multi-feature interactions may not decompose cleanly. MITIGATION: Prototype policy encoding early; iterate DP if needed.

ASSUMPTION: Worksite features are relatively static between baseline acquisition and mission execution (hours to days). RISK: Rapid site changes (e.g., equipment repositioning, debris accumulation) may invalidate baseline. MITIGATION: Define update frequency and change detection thresholds; trigger re-baseline if drift exceeds threshold.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP5 to explicitly specify: (1) Baseline model input format(s) and validation rules; (2) Sensor modalities and fusion strategy (e.g., visual SLAM + LiDAR for dense reconstruction vs. sparse feature tracking); (3) Feature taxonomy and attribute schema (geometry, classification, safety zone, confidence); (4) Delta encoding scheme (e.g., added/removed/modified features with timestamps); (5) Query interface contract (e.g., spatial range queries, feature-by-class, zone membership); (6) Confidence scoring model (e.g., detection confidence, localization uncertainty, temporal staleness). This will enable IM-level design (module boundaries, ownership, interfaces) and support evaluation of DP->IM mapping quality.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized spatial modeling and sensor fusion engine that maintains the hybrid baseline-plus-delta site model, manages confidence scoring, and coordinates real-time deviation detection across all vehicle observations.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time sensor observations (LiDAR, camera, IMU) from Vehicle; feeds raw observations into delta-encoding and confidence scoring pipeline.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator-provided baseline site model (blueprint/CAD) as prior; outputs updated spatial model with deviation flags and dynamic feature annotations to guide Vehicle navigation and task execution.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Emits alerts when detected deviations exceed confidence thresholds or dynamic features pose safety risks; triggers Safety Services intervention.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Transmits delta-encoded spatial updates (compressed) to Vehicle and Teleoperation; receives acknowledgments and supplementary sensor data from distributed nodes.

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit. NGHE Solution is the natural home for spatial modeling, sensor fusion, and baseline-delta reconciliation. The hybrid approach (baseline prior + real-time overlay) is a core NGHE capability. Confidence scoring and deviation detection are intrinsic to situational awareness. Minor diffusion: Vehicle must execute navigation based on model output; Teleoperation must visualize and validate deviations; Safety Services must act on alerts.

Poor fit. Vehicle is a mobile platform, not a centralized modeling engine. Placing the hybrid spatial model on-board would fragment the model across multiple vehicles, break coherence, and require expensive synchronization. Sensor fusion and baseline reconciliation belong upstream in NGHE, not distributed on each platform.

Weak fit. Teleoperation is a human-machine interface and command authority layer, not a spatial modeling engine. While operators provide the baseline model, the continuous fusion, delta-encoding, and confidence scoring are backend functions that must operate independently of operator interaction. High coupling to Vehicle and Safety Services.

Poor fit. Safety Services is a reactive enforcement layer that consumes alerts and enforces constraints. It does not own the spatial model or sensor fusion logic. Placing DP5 here inverts the dependency: Safety Services should react to deviations detected by NGHE, not generate them.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: DP5 (hybrid baseline-plus-delta spatial modeling with confidence scoring) is a core situational awareness function that belongs in the NGHE Solution layer. NGHE is the authoritative spatial reasoning engine; it ingests operator-provided priors (via Tasking), fuses real-time sensor observations (via Telemetry), maintains the delta-encoded model, and broadcasts deviations to downstream consumers (Vehicle for navigation, Teleoperation for visualization, Safety Services for constraint enforcement). This placement ensures model coherence, minimizes duplication, and establishes clear ownership of the fusion algorithm.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic spatial engine, it may accumulate unrelated modeling concerns (e.g., path planning, collision avoidance). Mitigation: Define clear boundaries between spatial state (owned by NGHE) and spatial reasoning (delegated to Vehicle or Safety Services).

Risk: Real-time latency. If NGHE Solution is centralized and remote, sensor-to-model update cycles may exceed Vehicle responsiveness requirements. Mitigation: Implement local caching on Vehicle; use delta-encoding and Network Link compression to minimize bandwidth; consider edge processing for high-frequency sensor streams.

Risk: Baseline model staleness. If operator-provided CAD is not regularly refreshed, the prior may diverge from ground truth, degrading confidence scores. Mitigation: Establish a feedback loop from NGHE to Teleoperation to flag when observed deviations are systematic (indicating baseline drift).

Risk: Confidence scoring is domain-specific (e.g., LiDAR vs. camera vs. thermal). If NGHE does not model sensor modality and environmental context, scores may be misleading. Mitigation: Parameterize confidence scoring by sensor type, lighting, occlusion, and mission phase.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH. The FR ('Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model') is directly verifiable against acceptance criteria: (1) worksite model/map is generated and available to planning/execution functions, and (2) agreed set of site features is captured. The IM boundary (NGHE Solution) is the authoritative owner of model generation and maintenance. Verification can be performed by: (a) confirming model availability to Vehicle and Tasking consumers, (b) validating feature coverage against baseline CAD, (c) measuring delta-encoding latency and compression ratios, (d) auditing confidence scores against ground truth samples.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: EXCELLENT. The allocation from FR to DP to IM is clear and traceable. FR specifies the problem (capture and represent features). DP5 specifies the solution approach (hybrid baseline-plus-delta with confidence scoring). IM (NGHE Solution) is the single authoritative owner of this capability. The external interfaces (Telemetry in, Tasking in/out, SafetyAlert out, Network Link in/out) establish unambiguous data flows and ownership boundaries. No ambiguity exists regarding which module is responsible for model coherence, fusion logic, or confidence scoring.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP5 is a sound and complete solution to FR. The hybrid approach (operator-provided baseline as prior + real-time sensor overlay + delta-encoding + confidence scoring) directly addresses the requirement to capture and represent worksite spatial features in machine-readable form. The DP is technology-agnostic (supports LiDAR, camera, IMU), scalable (delta-encoding minimizes bandwidth), and resilient (confidence scores enable downstream consumers to weight decisions appropriately). The DP is neither over-constrained (does not prescribe specific sensors or algorithms) nor under-constrained (specifies the logical structure: baseline, overlay, delta, confidence). Traceability to acceptance criteria is strong: the model is machine-readable (delta-encoded), available to planning/execution (via Tasking and Network Link interfaces), and captures agreed features (baseline CAD defines scope).

diffusion_risks: MODERATE: Sensor fusion algorithm ownership. While NGHE Solution owns the spatial model and confidence scoring framework, the actual sensor fusion algorithms (e.g., Kalman filtering, point cloud registration, feature matching) may be implemented as external libraries or third-party modules. If these algorithms are not clearly versioned and owned within NGHE, they risk becoming diffused across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Mitigation: Establish a 'Sensor Fusion Library' sub-module within NGHE Solution with explicit API contracts; version and test independently.

MODERATE: Real-time delta-encoding pipeline. The delta-encoding and confidence scoring pipeline is computationally intensive. If NGHE Solution does not clearly separate batch processing (baseline reconciliation) from streaming processing (real-time delta updates), the DP may diffuse into Vehicle (for edge caching) and Network Link (for compression negotiation). Mitigation: Define a clear streaming architecture within NGHE; specify which operations are synchronous (must complete within latency budget) and which are asynchronous (can be batched).

LOW-MODERATE: Baseline model versioning and update authority. The DP specifies that operator-provided baseline (blueprint/CAD) is a prior, but does not clarify who owns baseline versioning, approval workflows, or rollback procedures. If Teleoperation and NGHE Solution both claim authority over baseline updates, the DP diffuses. Mitigation: Establish a clear ownership model: Teleoperation provides baseline input; NGHE Solution maintains versioning and reconciliation logic; Safety Services approves baseline changes that affect safety constraints.

LOW: Confidence score parameterization. The DP specifies confidence scoring but does not detail how scores are parameterized by sensor modality, environmental context, or mission phase. If each consumer (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) implements its own confidence interpretation, the DP diffuses into domain-specific scoring logic. Mitigation: Define a canonical confidence scoring schema within NGHE; publish scoring parameters and interpretation guidelines to all consumers.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: EXCELLENT containment. The provided module_containment_scores analysis is thorough and correct. NGHE Solution achieves 0.92 containment with only 0.08 diffusion, reflecting strong architectural fit. The DP is intrinsically a centralized, backend function (spatial reasoning and model maintenance) that does not belong on distributed platforms (Vehicle) or human-facing interfaces (Teleoperation) or reactive enforcement layers (Safety Services). The four external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and establish clear producer-consumer relationships. The identified containment risks are valid and actionable: (1) monolithic creep (mitigate by separating spatial state from spatial reasoning), (2) latency (mitigate by edge caching and delta-encoding), (3) baseline staleness (mitigate by feedback loop), (4) confidence scoring domain-specificity (mitigate by parameterization). No recommended revisions to IM boundary are necessary; the architecture is sound.

recommended_revisions: REVISION 1 (DP Clarification): Extend DP5 to explicitly specify the confidence scoring model. Add: 'Confidence scores are parameterized by (a) sensor modality (LiDAR, camera, thermal, IMU), (b) environmental context (lighting, occlusion, weather), (c) feature type (static obstacle, dynamic object, surface), and (d) mission phase (initial survey, continuous monitoring, anomaly detection). Scores are normalized to [0, 1] and published with each feature in the delta-encoded model. Downstream consumers (Vehicle, Safety Services) interpret scores according to their risk tolerance and operational constraints.'

REVISION 2 (IM Sub-module Definition): Decompose NGHE Solution into explicit sub-modules to prevent diffusion: (a) 'Baseline Model Manager' (owns baseline CAD ingestion, versioning, approval workflows), (b) 'Sensor Fusion Engine' (owns real-time observation fusion, delta-encoding, confidence scoring), (c) 'Spatial State Store' (owns model persistence, query API, change notifications). Define clear interfaces between sub-modules and to external consumers.

REVISION 3 (Latency and Edge Processing): Add a constraint to DP5: 'Real-time sensor-to-model update latency must not exceed [X] ms (to be defined by Vehicle responsiveness requirements). If centralized NGHE processing exceeds this budget, implement local edge caching on Vehicle with eventual consistency reconciliation via Network Link.' This clarifies the boundary between NGHE (authoritative model) and Vehicle (local cache).

REVISION 4 (Baseline Feedback Loop): Add to DP5: 'If NGHE Solution detects systematic deviations between baseline model and observed features (e.g., >N% of features in a region exceed confidence threshold for >M consecutive updates), emit a 'Baseline Drift Alert' to Teleoperation. Teleoperation may trigger a baseline refresh cycle to re-survey the affected region and update CAD.' This closes the loop and prevents baseline staleness.

REVISION 5 (Acceptance Criteria Mapping): Explicitly map DP5 outputs to acceptance criteria. Add: 'Acceptance Criterion 1 (model availability): NGHE Solution publishes the spatial model via Tasking interface at [frequency] Hz; Vehicle and planning functions confirm receipt and model currency. Acceptance Criterion 2 (feature coverage): NGHE Solution ingests baseline CAD defining agreed features; model output includes all baseline features plus detected dynamic features; coverage is audited against baseline scope. Acceptance Criterion 3 (safety plan integration): NGHE Solution's SafetyAlert interface emits deviations that violate worksite safety plan constraints; Safety Services confirms alert receipt and enforcement action.'

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-IM, FR-DP, DP-IM) pass with high confidence, and the IM boundary (NGHE Solution) is well-contained (0.92 containment score). However, the recommended_revisions identify critical clarifications needed at the DP level before proceeding to sub-FR decomposition. Specifically: (1) confidence scoring parameterization is underspecified and risks domain-specific diffusion into Vehicle and Safety Services; (2) latency and edge-processing boundaries are not explicit, creating ambiguity about real-time responsiveness; (3) baseline versioning and feedback loops lack ownership clarity; (4) acceptance criteria mapping to DP outputs is incomplete. Given HIGH safety criticality, these gaps must be closed at the current level before decomposing into sub-FRs. Proceeding to decomposition without these revisions would propagate ambiguity into lower-level requirements and increase integration risk.

revise.why: DP5 (hybrid baseline-plus-delta with confidence scoring) is logically sound but requires explicit refinement in five areas to eliminate diffusion risks and clarify safety-critical interfaces: (1) Confidence Scoring Model: Define parameterization by sensor modality, environmental context, feature type, and mission phase; specify normalization and interpretation semantics for downstream consumers. (2) Latency and Edge Processing: Add explicit constraint on real-time sensor-to-model update latency; clarify boundary between centralized NGHE processing and Vehicle-local edge caching with eventual consistency reconciliation. (3) Baseline Versioning and Feedback: Establish ownership model for baseline CAD ingestion, versioning, approval workflows, and rollback; define baseline drift detection and refresh triggers. (4) Sub-module Decomposition: Decompose NGHE Solution into Baseline Model Manager, Sensor Fusion Engine, and Spatial State Store with explicit inter-module and external interfaces. (5) Acceptance Criteria Mapping: Explicitly trace DP5 outputs (model availability, feature coverage, safety alerts) to acceptance criteria with measurable verification methods. These revisions strengthen DP clarity without changing the fundamental solution approach and enable safe decomposition into sub-FRs.

assumptions: Operator-provided baseline (CAD/blueprint) is available and authoritative at mission start; baseline updates are infrequent but must be versioned and approved.
Real-time sensor fusion (LiDAR, camera, IMU) is computationally feasible within NGHE Solution's processing budget; delta-encoding reduces bandwidth to acceptable levels for Network Link.
Confidence scores are interpretable by downstream consumers (Vehicle, Safety Services) without domain-specific re-parameterization; a canonical scoring schema is sufficient.

Latency budget for sensor-to-model updates is compatible with Vehicle responsiveness requirements (e.g., <500 ms for obstacle detection); if not, edge caching on Vehicle is acceptable with eventual consistency.
Baseline drift (systematic deviations between baseline and observations) is detectable and actionable; Teleoperation can trigger baseline refresh cycles without disrupting ongoing missions.
Safety Services has authority to approve baseline changes that affect safety constraints; Teleoperation provides baseline input but does not unilaterally update safety-critical features.
open_risks: MODERATE: Confidence scoring parameterization complexity. If sensor modality, environmental context, and feature type parameters are not carefully designed, downstream consumers may implement incompatible scoring interpretations, leading to safety-critical misalignment (e.g., Vehicle trusts a low-confidence obstacle detection; Safety Services rejects it). Mitigation: Define canonical scoring schema with explicit interpretation guidelines; validate against Vehicle and Safety Services use cases before deployment.

MODERATE: Real-time latency budget feasibility. If NGHE Solution's centralized processing cannot meet latency requirements for time-critical features (e.g., dynamic obstacle detection), Vehicle may implement local fusion algorithms, diffusing the DP. Mitigation: Establish latency budget early; prototype delta-encoding pipeline; if budget is unmet, design edge-caching architecture with clear reconciliation semantics.

MODERATE: Baseline versioning and approval workflow complexity. If baseline update authority is ambiguous (Teleoperation vs. Safety Services vs. NGHE Solution), baseline staleness or safety-critical inconsistencies may occur. Mitigation: Establish clear ownership model with explicit approval gates; implement versioning and rollback procedures; audit baseline changes against safety constraints.

LOW-MODERATE: Sensor fusion library ownership and diffusion. If sensor fusion algorithms (Kalman filtering, point cloud registration) are not clearly owned and versioned within NGHE Solution, they risk being re-implemented or patched independently by Vehicle or Teleoperation. Mitigation: Establish 'Sensor Fusion Library' sub-module with explicit API contracts; version and test independently; publish API stability guarantees.

LOW: Confidence score parameterization domain-specificity. If Vehicle and Safety Services interpret confidence scores differently (e.g., Vehicle uses scores for path planning; Safety Services uses scores for constraint enforcement), the DP diffuses into domain-specific scoring logic. Mitigation: Define canonical interpretation semantics; publish scoring parameters and use-case examples; validate against all consumers.

LOW: Baseline model staleness during long-duration missions. If baseline is not refreshed and environmental changes occur (e.g., new obstacles, surface degradation), model accuracy degrades. Mitigation: Implement baseline drift detection and refresh triggers; establish feedback loop from NGHE to Teleoperation.
evidence_needed: Latency budget specification: Define maximum acceptable sensor-to-model update latency (e.g., <500 ms for dynamic obstacles, <5 s for static features) based on Vehicle responsiveness and safety requirements.

Confidence scoring schema design: Document parameterization by sensor modality, environmental context, feature type, and mission phase; specify normalization and interpretation semantics; validate against Vehicle path planning and Safety Services constraint enforcement use cases.

Baseline versioning and approval workflow: Define ownership model (Teleoperation input, NGHE Solution maintenance, Safety Services approval); specify versioning, rollback, and audit procedures; document baseline drift detection thresholds and refresh triggers.

Sensor fusion algorithm inventory: Identify all sensor fusion algorithms (Kalman filtering, point cloud registration, feature matching) and their current ownership; establish 'Sensor Fusion Library' sub-module with explicit API contracts and version control.

Edge-caching architecture (if latency budget is unmet): Design Vehicle-local caching strategy with eventual consistency reconciliation; specify cache invalidation and conflict resolution procedures; validate against Network Link bandwidth and latency constraints.

Acceptance criteria verification plan: Define measurable verification methods for model availability (e.g., model published at [frequency] Hz), feature coverage (e.g., all baseline features captured, coverage audited against CAD), and safety alert integration (e.g., SafetyAlert interface emits deviations, Safety Services confirms enforcement).

Safety-critical baseline change audit: Establish audit trail for baseline updates; confirm that all baseline changes affecting safety constraints are approved by Safety Services; validate that approval workflow prevents unauthorized or unsafe baseline modifications.
